title: Pseudoroegneria divaricata
taxonomy: Pseudoroegneria divaricata is classified as a species of grass within the genus Pseudoroegneria and the family Poaceae. It is a type of wild grass native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The genus Pseudoroegneria is a group of temperate grasses that are closely related to the genus Elymus. Pseudoroegneria divaricata is often used as a forage crop and for soil erosion control. This species is also known as Mongolian wildrye or narrowleaf bluegrass.
uses: Pseudoroegneria divaricata, also known as blue bunchgrass, is a type of grass native to North America. While there is limited information on its medicinal uses, it is reported to have some potential health benefits. The plant contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage.
